steps.title: Identify the structure after the blank
steps.content:
The phrase "the former establishing a Latino rights advocacy group and the latter legally affirming the rights of Latino students" is a noun phrase that renames and explains "civil rights history." This is a non-restrictive appositive — it adds extra information about the events already named. A comma is needed before it; no additional connecting word is required.
  • Choice A — 'history the': no comma before the appositive — the two clauses run together without any separation. ✗
  • Choice B — 'history, as the': adds "as," turning the appositive into an adverbial clause, which distorts the meaning. ✗
  • Choice C — 'history, the': a single comma correctly introduces the appositive phrase, matching standard punctuation for non-restrictive appositives. ✓
  • Choice D — 'history. The': a period creates two sentences, but "the former... the latter" has no complete verb and cannot stand alone as a sentence. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: A comma before "the" is the only punctuation that correctly attaches the appositive to the main clause without adding an incorrect word or breaking the sentence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
